D7 Print L4 Analyze Reference

SWOP vs FOGRA39

two coated-paper standards for offset look nearly identical at a glance — but ICC profiling chases the small paper-white shift and the few-ΔE drift in the solids

SWOP characterises US web/sheet-fed offset on coated stock; FOGRA39 (now FOGRA51) covers the European equivalent. Both target small ΔE on coated paper, but their reference paper white differs by a couple of b* units (FOGRA's stock is slightly cooler) and their CMYK solids land a few ΔE apart. ICC profiles encode those differences so the same image maps cleanly between the two presses.

The two columns print the same swatches under each profile. At 1× the differences are real but subtle — exactly as you'd see on press. Increase the exaggeration to make the paper-white shift and the small CMY-solid offsets obvious without changing the underlying numbers.
